content.text: SAIL domain has Boot configuration and this page is to analyze what is boot configuration and howe to use this in SAIL platform
Same like Option bytes in Renesas and BMHD (Boot Mode header) in Tricore. But QC BootCfg is controlled by GPIOs not via a Flash memory

rows: Can be controlled by SCC - SAIL_WDOG_OFF - P00.5 Debug board - WDOG_OFF
Selectsexternal boot device. SAILSS_BOOT_CONFIG_[3:1] 000 –OSPI 001 –QSPI 101 –QSPI (only for SoC ES2 and later) 111 –OSPI (only for SoC ES2 and later)
0 –Disables UEFI test manage r 1 –Enables UEFI test manager Note : This feature is not enabled in software, keep it at LOW

BIST_CONFIG_GPIO[1:0]00 –No BIST(ES1 devices) 01 –FULL BIST (ES2 and later devices) 10 –RESERVED 11 –RESERVED ![warning](https://inside-docupedia.bosch.com/confluence/plugins/servlet/twitterEmojiRedirector?id=26a0) Note: SKIP_MD_BIST and BIST_CONFIG_GPIO settings must be configured consistently. SKIP_MD_BIST = 0 with BIST_CONFIG_GPIO[1:0] = 01 SKIP_MD_BIST = 1 with BIST_CONFIG_GPIO[1:0] = 00

CLUSTER_CONFIG_GPIO[1:0] 00 –Cluster 0 split; Cluster 1 split 01 –Cluster 0 lock-step; Cluster 1 split 10 –Cluster 0 split; Cluster 1 lock-step 11 -Cluster 0 lock-step; cluster 1 lock-step ![warning](https://inside-docupedia.bosch.com/confluence/plugins/servlet/twitterEmojiRedirector?id=26a0) Check with software team to confirm which setting(s) could be used for a given release

content.text: Ensure that there are no unintentional external pulls on these SAIL_IOs. The external pulls can force the SA8775P device to enter the secure boot or select a different boot device
For initial bring-up purpose, ensure all SAILSS_BOOT_CONFIG pins are configured to the settings in red as default
SAIL_IO_37, SAIL_IO_39, SAIL_IO_43, SAIL_IO_45, and SAIL_IO_51 have internal default PD enabled on SA8775P ES1 samples and have internal default PU enabled on SA8775P ES2 and later samples.

rows: UART_RX in MD and SAIL domain
General purpose sideband interrupt between SAIL domain and VIP

content.text: Fast Mode and Device Programmer Mode are software features, For details, refer to QAM8775P IVI Boot and Corebsp Architecture Technical Overview

GPIO_35, SAIL_IO_5 and SAIL_IO_62 are used in Qualcomm reference platform by default. However, they are not hardware boot configuration pins, and the software functions can be reassigned to other GPIO or SAIL_IOs if required.
VIP needs to control the GPIO and SAIL_IOs into the right state based on the boot requirement during boot up. Both signals are sampled in the XBL stage
